title: carya myristiciformis
intro: Carya myristiciformis, also known as the Pignut Hickory or Bitternut Hickory, is a species of tree native to eastern North America, belonging to the family Juglandaceae. The bark and seeds of this tree have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Carya myristiciformis include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds. Carya myristiciformis can be prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infused oil, with some herbalists also using the bark and seeds in traditional herbal remedies.
